obligations.reason: The top module exposes debug_req_i and the CSR regfile contains debug_mode_q, debug_mode_o, set_debug_pc_o, dpc_q and DCSR-related CSRs. Check whether an external debug request can drive protected control-flow state without an authorization/lifecycle guard.
obligations.subject: external debug interface via debug_req_i
obligations.operation: enter debug mode / halt / redirect PC
obligations.object: debug mode state, debug PC redirection, halt control
obligations.expected_guard: debug authorization/lifecycle mediation before entering debug mode or changing PC/halt state; at minimum debug request should be gated by a permitted debug state and not only by pipeline/AMO conditions
obligations.candidate_path: debug_req_i -> debug_req -> csr_regfile.debug_req_i -> debug_mode_q/debug_mode_o/set_debug_pc_o/halt_csr_o -> controller/front-end PC redirect
state_conditions_to_check: interaction with amo_valid_commit and commit/flush state
whether debug_req_i is synchronized/gated by any auth/lifecycle signal
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: debug authorization or lifecycle permission
observed RTL guard/path including debug_req assignment and csr_regfile handling
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer is truncated around assignments; exact debug entry logic must be inspected in visible RTL.

obligations.reason: The package defines debug CSR addresses and csr_regfile contains dpc_q, dscratch0_q, dscratch1_q, debug_mode_q and csr read/write decode cases. Verify that normal CSR access cannot expose or modify debug state without debug-mode permission.
obligations.subject: software CSR access path from pipeline
obligations.expected_guard: debug CSRs at 0x7b0-0x7b3 should be readable/writable only in debug mode or by an authorized debug context; non-debug machine/supervisor/user CSR access should raise read/write access exception
obligations.candidate_path: CSR instruction interface csr_addr_i/csr_wdata_i/fu_op/commit_ack_i -> csr_we/csr_wdata -> CSR_DCSR/CSR_DPC/CSR_DSCRATCH0/CSR_DSCRATCH1 cases -> csr_rdata_o or *_q update
state_conditions_to_check: DRET handling and debug mode exit
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: debug_mode_q or equivalent debug authorization
observed read decode/write decode and exception behavior
source locations for CSR address cases and access exception logic
obligations.uncertainty: Need line-level CSR cases around reported unique case blocks to confirm guards.

obligations.reason: mstatus/sstatus fields control memory translation, supervisor user-memory access, trap behavior, and return semantics. The visible RTL has outputs for these fields used by decode/execute; verify CSR write mediation by current privilege and CSR address class.
obligations.subject: software CSR access path from pipeline
obligations.object: privilege and memory-access policy bits mstatus/sstatus: MPRV, SUM, MXR, TVM, TW, TSR, interrupt enables
obligations.expected_guard: writes to privilege-control fields in mstatus/sstatus should be permitted only at sufficient privilege; S-mode aliases must not modify machine-only fields (mprv/tvm/tw/tsr except architecturally allowed views) and U-mode must be denied
obligations.candidate_path: CSR instruction interface -> csr_addr_i decodes CSR_MSTATUS/CSR_SSTATUS -> mstatus fields mprv,sum,mxr,tvm,tw,tsr and outputs sum_o/mxr_o/tvm_o/tw_o/tsr_o/en_ld_st_translation_o
state_conditions_to_check: current privilege mode encoding from riscv_pkg
required_evidence_slots: subject: CSR instruction requester and current privilege state
expected guard: privilege level check based on CSR address/current mode
obligations.uncertainty: Current privilege signal names are not fully visible in facts; inspect CSR access-control logic.

obligations.reason: SATP outputs directly affect translation and address-space selection. Verify that lower privilege or TVM-prohibited contexts cannot alter translation policy, and that mode transitions do not leave stale TLB state active.
obligations.subject: software CSR access path from pipeline
obligations.object: SATP mode/asid/ppn and translation enable state
obligations.expected_guard: SATP and translation-enable changes require supervisor-or-machine privilege and must respect TVM restrictions; updates should be coupled with appropriate TLB flush/ordering so stale translations cannot bypass new policy
obligations.candidate_path: CSR_SATP write -> satp_ppn_o/asid_o/en_translation_o/en_ld_st_translation_o -> frontend/ex_stage/load-store translation and TLB flush path sfence_vma_commit_controller/flush_tlb_ctrl_ex
state_conditions_to_check: translation enable calculation across privilege modes
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: privilege and TVM check plus flush/ordering
observed write decode and flush_tlb_ctrl_ex path
impact: memory isolation bypass or stale mapping use
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect write case and controller connections for exact flush sequencing.

obligations.reason: The package defines PMP CSR addresses. Even if implementation is partial, verify whether these CSRs are decoded, whether privilege/lock checks exist, and whether fetch/load/store paths actually consume PMP policy or safely fault unsupported accesses.
obligations.subject: software CSR access path from pipeline
obligations.object: PMP configuration/address policy registers and downstream access-control checks
obligations.expected_guard: PMP configuration and address registers should be machine-mode only; lock bits should prevent later modification and PMP checks should mediate instruction fetch and load/store paths consistently
obligations.candidate_path: CSR writes to CSR_PMPCFG0/CSR_PMPADDR0 -> PMP configuration storage/use (if implemented) -> load/store/fetch access checks
state_conditions_to_check: existence of PMP state registers beyond fact excerpt
illegal CSR access behavior if PMP unsupported
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: machine privilege and sticky lock semantics
impact: memory access-control bypass if writable/ignored inconsistently
obligations.uncertainty: Facts show addresses but not PMP state; obligation includes confirming absence is handled safely.

obligations.reason: Machine CSRs hold trap vectors, exception PCs, delegation and interrupt state; unauthorized lower-privilege access could change control flow or expose machine state. The fact layer shows these registers and CSR address constants.
obligations.subject: software CSR access path from pipeline
obligations.object: machine-mode CSRs and trap/interrupt delegation state
obligations.expected_guard: machine CSRs should require machine privilege for writes and appropriate privilege/counter-enable guard for reads; lower privilege should receive illegal-access exception or masked view
obligations.candidate_path: CSR address decode for machine CSRs CSR_MSTATUS/MEDELEG/MIDELEG/MIE/MTVEC/MSCRATCH/MEPC/MCAUSE/MTVAL/MIP and counters -> csr_rdata_o/csr_we
state_conditions_to_check: exception return/trap update paths that also write these CSRs
required_evidence_slots: subject: CSR requester with current privilege
expected guard: machine privilege or architecturally allowed masked read
observed access exception/read mux/write enable behavior
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect access-control block near csr_regfile read/write decode.

obligations.reason: Supervisor CSRs influence control flow on traps and returns. Verify that U-mode cannot program supervisor trap state or return PCs and that SRET/MRET handling is mediated by current privilege and status bits.
obligations.subject: software CSR access path from pipeline
obligations.operation: read/write supervisor CSRs and return from trap
obligations.object: supervisor trap vectors, exception return PC, scratch/cause/value and interrupt enable/pending state
obligations.expected_guard: supervisor CSRs should be inaccessible from U-mode; S-mode access should obey machine control bits (TVM/TW/TSR where relevant) and delegation semantics; trap return should not allow privilege escalation
obligations.candidate_path: CSR write to stvec/sepc/sscratch/scause/stval/sie/sip/sstatus -> trap_vector_base_o/epc_o/eret_o and delegation/interrupt logic
state_conditions_to_check: trap entry updates vs explicit CSR writes
reset defaults of supervisor trap state
required_evidence_slots: operation: read/write supervisor CSRs or SRET
expected guard: supervisor-or-higher privilege and TSR/return checks
obligations.uncertainty: Privilege tracking signals not fully enumerated in facts; inspect csr_regfile state and decode.

obligations.reason: Counters and performance events can expose timing/microarchitectural activity across privilege domains. The visible RTL includes cycle_q, instret_q, perf_* ports and many PERF CSR constants; check enable guards for user/supervisor reads and write mediation.
obligations.subject: software CSR access path and perf counter interface
obligations.expected_guard: counter/performance reads from lower privilege should be controlled by counter-enable policy (e.g., mcounteren/scounteren or equivalent); writes to machine counters/perf config should require machine privilege
obligations.candidate_path: CSR counter/performance read path CSR_CYCLE/CSR_TIME/CSR_INSTRET and PERF_* CSRs -> csr_rdata_o; perf_addr_o/perf_we_o/perf_data_o -> perf_counters
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: counter-enable and privilege checks
observed read mux/write enable to perf_counters
impact: timing/performance information exposure or counter tampering
obligations.uncertainty: Whether counter-enable CSRs are implemented must be verified in csr_regfile.

obligations.reason: Custom cache CSRs CSR_ICACHE and CSR_DCACHE drive icache_en_o/dcache_en_o and cache-control paths. Verify they are not writable by unprivileged CSR instructions and that state changes are safely flushed/ordered.
obligations.subject: software CSR access path
obligations.operation: configure cache policy / trigger cache control effects
obligations.object: instruction/data cache enable/configuration state and flush effects
obligations.expected_guard: cache enable/disable and flush/configuration CSRs should be restricted to privileged software; lower privilege must not be able to alter cache policy or trigger privileged cache effects
obligations.candidate_path: CSR write to CSR_DCACHE/CSR_ICACHE -> dcache_q/icache_q -> dcache_en_o/icache_en_o and cache flush signals
required_evidence_slots: observed decode/write enable and downstream use
impact: denial of service, stale instruction/data policy effects, isolation side effects
obligations.uncertainty: Custom CSR privilege class must be inferred from decode/access-control logic.

obligations.reason: The CSR regfile exposes many internal control-flow and scratch registers through a common csr_rdata_o path. Verify the read-access exception is applied before data is returned/committed so lower privilege cannot observe protected state even transiently through pipeline writeback.
obligations.subject: software CSR read path
obligations.object: mscratch/mepc/mcause/mtval, sscratch/sepc/scause/stval, dpc/dscratch and other internal state exposed via csr_rdata_o
obligations.expected_guard: read data from higher-privilege or debug-only state should be blocked, exceptioned, or masked when requester privilege is lower than the CSR privilege class
obligations.candidate_path: CSR read mux -> csr_rdata_o for scratch/epc/cause/tval registers across M/S/debug domains
state_conditions_to_check: read_access_exception asserted while csr_rdata_o carries protected value
whether downstream commit suppresses rdata on exception
debug_mode_q false reads of debug CSRs
required_evidence_slots: object: protected CSR register selected in read mux
observed read mux ordering relative to read_access_exception and commit/writeback
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect commit_stage behavior if csr_rdata_o may be produced alongside exception.

obligations.reason: Return instructions change privilege and PC. The fact layer shows mret/sret/dret signals and eret_o/epc_o outputs; check that illegal lower-mode returns cannot cause control-flow or privilege transitions.
obligations.subject: software instruction execution path
obligations.object: privilege state, debug_mode_q, epc_o/dpc_q and eret control
obligations.expected_guard: return instructions should be legal only in the corresponding privilege/debug mode; DRET only in debug mode, SRET subject to TSR and supervisor privilege, MRET only machine privilege; returned mode/PC must be derived from protected status fields
obligations.candidate_path: MRET/SRET/DRET decode via fu_op -> mret/sret/dret -> privilege/debug mode updates, eret_o, epc_o, debug_mode_q clearing
required_evidence_slots: subject: instruction stream at current privilege/debug mode
object: privilege/debug state and return PC outputs
impact: privilege escalation or unauthorized debug exit/entry
obligations.uncertainty: Exact current privilege register not included in fact excerpt; inspect csr_regfile sequential update logic.

obligations.reason: FP status/control fields can be accessed through low-numbered CSRs and also via commit-side fflags writes. Verify permission checks and state-update paths are consistent so disabled FP contexts cannot alter protected FP state.
obligations.subject: software CSR path and FPU commit path
obligations.operation: read/write FP control/status and update dirty state
obligations.object: FCSR/FFLAGS/FRM/FP precision and mstatus FS dirty state
obligations.expected_guard: floating-point CSR updates and dirty-state transitions should be allowed only when FP is enabled for current privilege; illegal FP-disabled contexts should not modify FP status or expose stale FP state
obligations.candidate_path: csr_write_fflags_i/dirty_fp_state_i and FCSR/FFLAGS/FRM writes -> fflags_o/frm_o/fprec_o and mstatus.fs/dirty state
required_evidence_slots: subject: CSR requester or FPU commit path
expected guard: FP enable/current privilege policy
observed CSR decode plus csr_write_fflags_i/dirty_fp_state_i behavior
impact: unauthorized FP state modification or stale-state exposure
obligations.uncertainty: FP enable policy may be in id_stage/ex_stage; inspect connections around csr outputs.

obligations.reason: The top module instantiates instruction tracing and contains PC/cycle queue signals. Check whether trace outputs are simulation-only, conditionally compiled, or externally reachable, and whether they leak privileged control flow without authorization.
obligations.subject: trace/debug observation path
obligations.object: program counter trace, cycle trace, instruction trace visibility
obligations.expected_guard: instruction trace or PC/cycle exposure should be disabled or authorization-gated in production/security modes, especially during privileged/debug execution if outputs leave the core boundary
obligations.candidate_path: top-level tracer instances tracer_if/instr_tracer_i/instr_tracer and signals piton_pc_vld/piton_pc/pc_queue/cycles -> external trace/log interface
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: debug/trace authorization or synthesis exclusion
obligations.uncertainty: Facts do not show preprocessor guards or outputs; inspect ariane.sv tracer section.
